title: [ipv6_addr.c:261] snprintf return value cast to size_t may wrap
description: At line 261, (size_t)ret is compared to buflen. But at line 252, ret < 0 is checked and handled. The remaining code at 261 assumes ret >= 0. This is correct, but ret is int, and casting a large positive int to size_t and comparing to buflen (also size_t) is fine. However, the comment says 'defensive - buflen check above should prevent this' but the buflen check at line 226 ensures buflen >= 40, and snprintf returns 39 (the fixed format produces exactly 39 chars). So this check can never trigger. This is defensive but dead code.
close_reason: Fixed: Added comment clarifying the truncation check is defensive dead code
